etymology.description: 爪('hands') + 角 Phonetic ('horn') + 女('woman') -> Later: 母('mother') + 中('middle') + 女('daughter')
etymology.interpretation: The character 婁 originally combined semantic 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hands") and phonetic 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn") with the semantic 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") to possibly indicate some aspect of interaction or action involving women. Its later reanalysis as an ideogrammic compound with 母(母) (mǔ, "mother") and 女(女) (nǚ, "daughter") suggests a familial or generational context. The original meaning remains uncertain but was likely related to these components.
